missingUniversalFields.reason: Need normalized category separate from UI family.
Professional logic needs insert, solid drill, indexable drill, tap, reamer, end mill, holder, etc.
Code alone is not a stable SKU.
Cross-reference and compare require canonical designations.
Insert/tap/drill/end mill codes must be decoded into comparable geometry attributes.
Single ISO letter is insufficient; tools may support multiple groups with different cutting data.
Vc/feed/depth must be scoped to material, operation and tool diameter/context.
Feed and depth fields currently mean different things by category.
Present in legacy schema but missing in active TA_TOOLS records.
Grade comparison needs substrate and coating distinction.
Machine, coolant, rigidity and interrupted-cut constraints drive real selection.
Professional recommendations need negative constraints, not only bestFor text.
Current source is only a label; confidence needs evidence.
Need per-field confidence instead of one product-level score.
Synthetic unitPrice/costTier need real currency, pack size and date.
Supply count is not enough for procurement.

weakCurrentFields.issue: String values mix category and workflow, and some records appear misclassified.
Flat values are not comparable across turning, milling, drilling, threading and reaming.
Insert-specific; solid categories use '-' and lose geometry.
Single ISO group only; no primary/secondary or material-specific data.
Rendered as mm/rev even for milling and threading.
Meaning changes by category and is invalid for some drill/tap/reamer contexts.
Single score with no evidence, field breakdown, source count or stale-data handling.
Coarse label only; no URL, document, date, field mapping or reviewer.
Synthetic economics; should be flagged as estimated and excluded from verified comparisons.
Derived only from same shape, ISO and family; insufficient for real cross-reference.
